chapterTitle: Claude in action: use-cases by role

questions.question: Which of the following is a use case that applies across many professional roles and industries?
options: Generating project status reports
Building a battle card library
Drafting investment memos
Planning a literature review
questions.explanation: Generating project status reports is listed under general professional use cases that apply across many roles and industries. The other options are specific to sales (battle cards), finance (investment memos), and research (literature reviews) respectively.

questions.question: What is one way marketing professionals can use Claude to improve content efficiency?
options: Adapt content across platforms for different channels and audiences
Create new hire onboarding guides
Build financial models
Organize case timelines
questions.explanation: Adapting content across platforms is a specific marketing use case that allows marketers to efficiently repurpose content for different channels and audiences. The other options belong to HR, finance, and legal roles respectively.

questions.question: Which role would benefit most from Claude's ability to track discovery timelines and analyze patterns?
options: Sales professionals
Legal professionals
Finance professionals
Marketing professionals
questions.explanation: Legal professionals specifically use Claude to track discovery timelines and analyze patterns in legal documents. This is a core function in legal work where managing complex case information is essential.

questions.question: A sales manager wants to help the team prepare for upcoming client meetings. Which Claude use case would be most appropriate?
options: Analyze campaign performance data
Prepare for sales deals by researching prospects and organizing talking points
Create new hire onboarding guides
Verify statistics from raw data
questions.explanation: Preparing for sales deals by researching prospects and organizing talking points directly supports sales professionals in getting ready for important meetings. The other options serve marketing, HR, and research roles respectively.

questions.question: A finance team needs to understand a complex spreadsheet that was created by a departing employee. Which Claude use case addresses this need?
options: Build financial models
Draft investment memos
Understand and extend an inherited spreadsheet
Analyze patterns in user feedback
questions.explanation: Understanding and extending an inherited spreadsheet is specifically designed to help finance professionals decode complex spreadsheets and add new functionality. This directly addresses the scenario of inheriting unfamiliar spreadsheet work.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how Claude can support competitive positioning in sales?
options: By creating comprehensive onboarding materials for new sales hires
By building a battle card library that serves as competitive intelligence resources
By analyzing campaign performance metrics across channels
By organizing case timelines and legal documents
questions.explanation: Building a battle card library creates competitive intelligence resources that help sales teams win deals by understanding competitive positioning. This is a sales-specific use case for competitive advantage, distinct from onboarding, marketing analytics, or legal work.

questions.question: An HR director wants to improve the experience for new employees joining different departments. What Claude capability would be most valuable?
options: Creating new hire onboarding guides tailored to different roles
Analyzing patterns in user feedback from exit surveys
Building financial models for headcount planning
Tracking discovery timelines for compliance documentation
questions.explanation: Creating new hire onboarding guides tailored to different roles directly supports HR's goal of developing comprehensive, role-specific onboarding materials. While feedback analysis could inform improvements, the primary HR use case for new employee experience is structured onboarding guide creation.

questions.question: A researcher is concerned about the accuracy of statistical calculations in their analysis. Which Claude use case would help address this concern?
options: Plan your literature review
Verify statistics from raw data
Analyze campaign performance
Build financial models
questions.explanation: Verifying statistics from raw data allows researchers to double-check calculations and statistical analyses for accuracy. This directly addresses quality assurance in research work, whereas literature review planning is about organizing sources rather than validating calculations.

questions.question: A marketing director needs to understand why a recent campaign underperformed and wants to make data-driven decisions for the next campaign. Which use case is most relevant?
options: Adapt content across platforms
Analyze campaign performance to extract insights from campaign metrics
Generate project status reports
Create sales reports from pipeline data
questions.explanation: Analyzing campaign performance extracts insights from campaign metrics to inform strategy, which directly supports understanding underperformance and making data-driven decisions. While content adaptation is a marketing use case, it addresses distribution rather than performance analysis.

questions.question: A finance professional wants to create a reusable tool that applies company-specific financial standards and methodologies to multiple projects. Which approach would best leverage Claude's capabilities?
options: Build individual financial models for each project separately
Draft investment memos for each analysis
Package financial standards in a Claude skill that can be reused across projects
Understand and extend inherited spreadsheets
questions.explanation: While the source mentions packaging brand guidelines in a skill as a general professional use case, this same principle applies to finance—creating a reusable Claude skill that applies company-specific standards ensures consistency and efficiency across multiple projects. This is more scalable than building individual models or memos for each project.

questions.question: A legal team is managing a complex case with multiple documents and needs to identify key patterns and relationships between events. Which combination of Claude capabilities would be most effective?
options: Track discovery timelines and analyze patterns in legal documents
Analyze patterns in user feedback and generate status reports
Build financial models and draft investment memos
Adapt content across platforms and create onboarding guides
questions.explanation: Tracking discovery timelines and analyzing patterns directly addresses the legal team's need to organize complex case information and identify key relationships. The other combinations mix unrelated use cases from different professional domains that would not serve legal discovery work.

questions.question: An organization wants to implement Claude across multiple departments to improve efficiency. Which strategy would ensure the most relevant and impactful adoption?
options: Deploy the same use cases uniformly across all departments regardless of their function
Identify role-specific use cases that align with each department's core responsibilities and workflows
Focus exclusively on general professional use cases that apply across all roles
Implement only the most technically complex use cases to maximize Claude's capabilities
questions.explanation: Identifying role-specific use cases ensures that Claude implementation aligns with each department's actual needs and workflows, maximizing adoption and impact. While general professional use cases provide baseline value, role-specific applications (sales battle cards, finance models, legal timeline tracking, etc.) deliver the most relevant benefits. Uniform deployment or focusing only on general cases would miss significant opportunities for departmental efficiency gains.
